rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical triacetoxylation of allylbenzenes (alkylbenzenes bearing a terminal alkene) to give 1,2,3-triacetoxy products via anodic oxidation and acetate trapping
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of the allylarene to an alkene radical cation; subsequent anodic oxidation of a β-acetoxy carbon radical to a cation/acyloxonium; cathode reduces protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: 1,3-dioxolan-2-ylium (acyloxocarbenium) intermediate D with Me at C2
benzylic radical F after further anodic oxidation/H loss

electron_flow: The anode performs SET on the allylarene to generate an alkene radical cation that is captured by acetate to give a β-acetoxy benzylic radical; a second anodic oxidation produces a cation that closes to a methyl-substituted 1,3-dioxolan-2-ylium, which is opened by acetate to the 1,2-diacetate, and a further anodic oxidation/deprotonation followed by acetate trapping installs the third OAc. Protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: 1,3-diacetoxy isomer 6a (putative intermediate)
probe_reagents.observation: Resubjection of 6a under standard conditions gave 2a in 68%; without electricity 2a was not detected — supports 6a as a competent intermediate requiring anodic oxidation

probe_reagents.name: cinnamyl acetate 7a
probe_reagents.observation: Decomposed under standard conditions, no 2a formed — excludes allylic acetate H as on-cycle intermediate

probe_reagents.name: internal alkene β-methylstyrene 1a'
probe_reagents.observation: Gave 1,2-diacetate 3a in 75% but no triacetate 2a — suggests terminal alkene geometry is required for triacetoxylation

probe_reagents.name: para-CF3 allylbenzene 1p
probe_reagents.observation: Gave 2p (22%) and 1,3-diacetate 3p (6%); electron-poor arene lowers yield, consistent with arene-assisted oxidation

probe_reagents.name: cyclopropyl-substituted allylarene (radical clock)
probe_reagents.observation: No ring-opened products from a vinylcyclopropane substrate; ring-opened acyclic triacetate 2v formed in 7% (1.4:1 dr), consistent with a radical intermediate adjacent to the cyclopropane

probe_reagents.name: in situ GC-MS time course
probe_reagents.observation: GC-MS at 2.5 h detected 1,2-diacetate, 1,3-diacetate (6a), propylbenzene/alkene byproducts, and triacetate 2a, supporting stepwise build-up via diacetate intermediates

notes: Authors disfavor a pathway through cinnamyl acetate H (allylic C–H acetoxylation product), since independently prepared 7a decomposes under the conditions and does not yield 2a. The 1,3-diacetate 6a is shown to be a productive on-cycle intermediate (requires current), implicating a 1,3-dioxolan-2-ylium (acyloxonium) opening by acetate. Internal alkene 1a' diverts to the 1,2-diacetate 3a only, indicating the third acetoxylation requires the terminal-alkene-derived radical/cation manifold. A minor amount of ring-opened triacetate from a cyclopropyl probe (2v, 7%) supports radical character at the benzylic position.
